question: What is the legal name of your company?
helperText: Provide the exact legal name as registered with the government authorities (e.g., Registrar of Companies).
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Company Name
guidanceTips: The company name should match the name on your GST registration, PAN card, and other official documents.
Avoid using abbreviations, acronyms, or informal names. Use the full legal name of the business.
If your company has multiple locations, use the name of the headquarters or primary place of business.

question: What is your business registration number?
helperText: This is the unique identification number assigned to your business by the government. It is typically a combination of letters and numbers.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Business Registration Number
guidanceTips: The business registration number is typically a 10-15 character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies your business.
Common examples include the Goods and Services Tax (GST) Identification Number (GSTIN) or the Permanent Account Number (PAN) for your business.
Make sure to enter the exact number as it appears on your business registration documents. Typos or incorrect entries may delay the processing of your insurance application.

question: What is your business's Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number?
helperText: The GST number is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned to your business by the Indian government. It is mandatory for all businesses registered under the GST Act.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for providing your GST number
guidanceTips: Ensure you enter the exact 15-digit GST number as it appears on your GST registration certificate.
The GST number follows a specific format: XX-AAAAA-NNNN-Z-Y-Z, where XX is the state code, AAAAA is the PAN-based business entity number, NNNN is a unique number, and Z and Y are check digits.
If you have recently registered for GST, make sure to use the new GST number and not the old VAT/Service Tax registration number.

question: What is your company's Permanent Account Number (PAN)?
helperText: The PAN is a unique 10-digit alphanumeric number issued by the Income Tax Department of India. It is mandatory for all businesses to have a valid PAN for tax and regulatory purposes.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing PAN Number
guidanceTips: The PAN number should be entered exactly as it appears on your company's registration documents or tax filings.
Ensure that there are no typos or errors in the PAN number, as it is a critical identifier for your business.
If you do not have a PAN number yet, you will need to apply for one before proceeding with the insurance application.

question: What is the primary business type or industry of your company?
helperText: Select the option that best describes your primary business activities.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Selecting Business Type/Industry
guidanceTips: Choose the industry that most closely matches your company's core business operations.
If your business activities span multiple industries, select the one that accounts for the majority of your revenue or operations.
If your business type is not listed, select 'Other' and provide a brief description in the next field.

question: What is the year your business was established?
helperText: Enter the 4-digit year your business was officially registered or started operations.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Year of Establishment
guidanceTips: Enter the exact 4-digit year your business was established, e.g. 2015.
If you're not sure of the exact year, provide your best estimate based on when your business started operations.
The year of establishment is an important detail for insurance providers to understand the history and longevity of your business.

question: What is the total number of employees in your business?
placeholder: Enter the exact number of employees
helperText: Provide the current total number of employees in your business. This information is required to calculate the appropriate surety insurance coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Providing the Number of Employees
guidanceTips: Count all full-time, part-time, and contractual employees in your business.
Ensure the number you provide is the exact current total, not a range.
This information is crucial for the insurance provider to assess the risk and provide an accurate quote for your surety insurance policy.

question: What is your company's total annual revenue?
placeholder: Enter your annual revenue in INR
helperText: Please provide your company's total revenue for the last financial year. This information is required to calculate the appropriate surety bond coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Annual Revenue
guidanceTips: Enter the exact annual revenue figure, not a range. This helps us provide the most accurate surety insurance quote.
Make sure to include all sources of revenue, including sales, services, and any other business income.
If your company's fiscal year is different from the calendar year, provide the revenue for the most recently completed fiscal year.

question: What is the registered address of your business?
helperText: Provide the full address where your business operations are based, including the building name, street, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
guidanceTitle: Tips for Entering Your Business Address
guidanceTips: Make sure to include all relevant address details like building name, street, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
Ensure the address matches your company's registration documents like GST and PAN.
If your business operates from multiple locations, provide the primary registered address.

question: What is the city where your business is located?
helperText: Provide the exact city name where your business operations are based.
guidanceTitle: Entering the City
guidanceTips: Enter the full city name, not abbreviations or short forms.
Ensure the spelling is accurate as per official records.
If your business has multiple locations, provide the primary city address.

question: In which state is your business registered?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
helperText: Select the state where your business is registered.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Selecting the State
guidanceTips: Ensure that the state selected matches the registered address of your business.
If your business has operations in multiple states, select the state where the headquarters or primary place of business is located.
The state selected should be consistent with the information provided in your GST registration and other official documents.

question: What is your business's PIN (Postal Index Number) code?
helperText: The PIN code is a 6-digit number used by the Indian postal service to identify geographic areas. It is required for insurance policy issuance and delivery.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for providing the correct PIN code
guidanceTips: The PIN code should be a valid 6-digit number without any leading zeros.
You can find your business's PIN code by entering your address on the India Post website or by contacting your local post office.
Ensure the PIN code you provide matches the location of your business operations, as this information is used to assess risk and determine insurance coverage.

question: Who is the primary contact person for this insurance application?
placeholder: Enter full name of contact person
helperText: Provide the name of the person who will be the main point of contact for this insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Contact Person Name
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the primary contact person, including first, middle, and last name.
This should be an authorized representative of the business who has the authority to make decisions about the insurance policy.
Ensure the name matches the government-issued ID (such as PAN card) that will be provided for verification.

question: What is your official business email address?
helperText: Please provide the email address registered with your business. This will be used for all communication regarding your surety insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Entering your Business Email Address
guidanceTips: Use the official email address associated with your registered business, not a personal email.
Ensure the email address is spelled correctly and does not contain any typos.
If you have multiple business email addresses, provide the one you use for official correspondence.

question: What is your business's primary contact phone number?
placeholder: Enter your 10-digit mobile or landline number
helperText: Please provide a valid 10-digit phone number where you can be reached.
guidanceTitle: Providing Your Business Phone Number
guidanceTips: Enter your primary business contact number, which can be a mobile or landline.
The number should be 10 digits long and start with 6, 7, 8, or 9.
Ensure the number provided is active and regularly monitored, as the insurance provider may need to contact you regarding your policy.

question: When would you like the surety insurance policy to start?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select a start date within the next 12 months.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Selecting Policy Start Date
guidanceTips: Choose a start date that aligns with your business needs and operations.
Ensure the start date is at least a few days after the current date to allow for processing.
Consider any upcoming projects, contracts, or financial obligations that may require surety coverage.

question: When does the surety insurance policy need to end?
helperText: Enter the date when the surety insurance policy should end. The policy period is typically 1 year.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the Policy End Date
guidanceTips: The policy end date should be exactly 1 year after the policy start date.
Ensure the end date is a valid calendar date and not a weekend or holiday.
The end date must be within the current or next calendar year, as per industry standards.

question: What is the total coverage amount required for your surety insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter the coverage amount in INR
helperText: The coverage amount should be between ₹1 lakh and ₹5 crores, as per industry standards.
guidanceTitle: Determining the Appropriate Coverage Amount
guidanceTips: Consider the value of the contracts or projects you need to be covered by the surety insurance.
Factor in the potential risks and liabilities associated with your business operations.
Consult with your insurance advisor to determine the optimal coverage amount based on your specific requirements.
Keep in mind that the coverage amount should be high enough to protect your business, but not unnecessarily high, as it will impact the premium.

question: Who is your current surety insurance provider?
placeholder: Enter the name of your current surety insurance provider
helperText: Provide the full legal name of your current surety insurance provider.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Current Surety Insurance Provider
guidanceTips: Enter the complete legal name of the insurance company that currently provides your surety coverage.
Avoid abbreviations or short forms - provide the full official name as it appears on your policy documents.
If you have multiple surety policies with different providers, enter the name of the provider for your primary or largest surety policy.

question: Please provide details of any previous insurance claims made by your business in the last 5 years.
helperText: Provide a brief summary of any insurance claims made, including the claim amount, date, and reason.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Previous Claims History
guidanceTips: Be as detailed and accurate as possible when describing previous claims. This information helps the insurer assess your risk profile.
Include the policy number, claim amount, date of incident, and a short description of the reason for the claim.
If you have not made any previous claims, simply state 'No previous claims' in the field.

question: Please describe the risk management measures in place for your business operations.
placeholder: Provide details on your risk management policies, procedures, and controls
helperText: Explain the key risk management practices you have implemented to mitigate potential risks in your business.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Describing Risk Management Measures
guidanceTips: Outline the specific policies, processes, and controls you have in place to identify, assess, and manage risks in your business operations.
Provide details on how you monitor and review your risk management measures on a regular basis to ensure their effectiveness.
Highlight any industry-specific risk management standards or certifications your business has obtained, such as ISO 31000 or COSO ERM.
